title: Stellar Privy signer — Privy-custodied co-signer (additive to Wave 4)
goal: Add a Privy-custodied co-signer (PrivyStellarCosigner) implementing the Wave 4 CompassStellarCosigner contract via Privy server-wallet raw-sign (Ed25519), selectable with COMPASS_STELLAR_SIGNER_PROVIDER=local|privy. Same policy gate, binding, testnet guard, and fail-closed config as Wave 4; the co-signer key is custodied by Privy and never held by Compass.

context.analysisSource: PRODUCT_CONSTITUTION.md names a PrivyAdapter as the intended production signer. Wave 4 proved the co-signer with a raw local seed (COMPASS_STELLAR_SIGNER_SECRET). Privy supports Stellar via Ed25519 raw-sign: privy.wallets().rawSign(walletId, { params: { hash } }) using @privy-io/node and PrivyClient({ appId, appSecret }).

openQuestions.question: Fetch the wallet public key from Privy at runtime, or configure it?
openQuestions.resolution: DECIDED: Configure it via COMPASS_STELLAR_PRIVY_WALLET_PUBLIC_KEY. The public key is not secret, configuring it avoids an extra API round-trip per cosign, and it keeps the adapter offline-testable.

openQuestions.question: Hard-depend on @privy-io/node, or structurally type the client?
openQuestions.resolution: DECIDED: Define a minimal structural PrivyWalletClient interface and inject it; a lazy factory builds the real @privy-io/node client at runtime. Tests inject a fake client so they need no Privy package or credentials.

acceptanceCriteria: PrivyStellarCosigner satisfies CompassStellarCosigner and is selectable via COMPASS_STELLAR_SIGNER_PROVIDER=privy (default local).
The attached signature is a valid Stellar Ed25519 signature over tx.hash() for the configured wallet public key (verifiable by the Stellar SDK).
Gating (ALLOW-only), envelope-to-candidate binding, testnet-only guard, and fail-closed config behave exactly as the Wave 4 local signer.
The Privy app secret never appears in any CosignResult, audit metadata, or log.
The brain and Solana are untouched; existing tests stay green.
nonGoals: No change to Wave 4 local signer behavior (Privy is additive/opt-in).
No Privy user-side embedded wallet / login flow.
No real Privy network call in CI (manual, like the Wave 6 testnet run).
verification: Manual: with a real Privy app + Stellar server wallet, COMPASS_STELLAR_SIGNER_PROVIDER=privy, run scripts/stellar-demo.mjs and confirm Compass co-signs via Privy on ALLOW cases.

tasks.title: Add RED tests for the Privy co-signer
tasks.description: Write tests, before implementation, mocking the Privy client with a real Ed25519 keypair and asserting a valid attached signature, ALLOW-only gating, binding, fail-closed config, mainnet refusal, and secret non-exposure.
testsSpec: rawSign mock (real Ed25519) -> attached signature verifies against the wallet public key over tx.hash()
non-ALLOW decision -> POLICY_NOT_ALLOWED and rawSign not called
fingerprint mismatch -> ENVELOPE_CANDIDATE_MISMATCH and rawSign not called
no legacy/ import in new files

tasks.title: Implement the Privy client interface + lazy factory
tasks.description: Create privyClient.ts: structural PrivyWalletClient interface and a lazy factory that builds the real @privy-io/node client from PRIVY_APP_ID/PRIVY_APP_SECRET when not injected.

tasks.title: Implement PrivyStellarCosigner
tasks.description: Create privyStellarCosigner.ts implementing CompassStellarCosigner: gate on ALLOW, enforce binding, resolve config (fail-closed + mainnet guard), raw-sign tx.hash() via Privy, attach with tx.addSignature, return XDR.

tasks.title: Implement the provider selector
tasks.description: Create stellarCosignerFactory.ts: resolveStellarCosigner(env, deps) returning local (Wave 4) or privy by COMPASS_STELLAR_SIGNER_PROVIDER; default local. Add the env vars to .env.example.

tasks.title: Validate and pause for review
tasks.description: Run the full validation suite; confirm the brain and Solana are untouched and all existing tests stay green.

implementationEvidence.mode: Privy-custodied co-signer; additive to Wave 4; brain & Solana untouched
commands: npm run test:back -> 419 passed (46 files), incl. 9 new Privy tests
npm run test:back -- back/services/stellar/signer -> 21 passed (12 Wave4 + 9 Privy)
tsc --noEmit -> only the 1 PRE-EXISTING mcpProxyDispatcher error remains
deviations: PrivyStellarCosigner implements the SAME Wave 4 CompassStellarCosigner contract; only the signing step differs (Privy raw-sign of tx.hash() Ed25519, attached via tx.addSignature). Gate/binding/testnet-guard/fail-closed reused from Wave 4.
The key Privy test backs the rawSign mock with a REAL Ed25519 keypair, so the attached signature genuinely verifies (2 sigs on the tx, Privy hint present) — not a stub.
@privy-io/node imported via dynamic import() inside a lazy factory (eslint no-require-imports compliant); tests inject a fake client and never load the package.
Wallet public key is configured (COMPASS_STELLAR_PRIVY_WALLET_PUBLIC_KEY), not fetched, to avoid an extra round-trip and keep the adapter offline-testable.
Real Privy network call needs a Privy app + Stellar server wallet; kept manual (like the Wave 6 testnet run), not in CI.
